headline: Citizen Moana Watch Shows Disney Extending Franchises Into Daily Life
summary: Citizen has launched its first Disney Moana themed timepiece, aligned with the next chapter of the Moana franchise. The watch focuses on story driven design and collectible appeal, expanding Disney's presence in lifestyle and luxury merchandise. This collaboration highlights another way Walt Disney (NYSE:DIS) uses character licensing to reach consumers beyond traditional media. headline: Roku For Sale? JPMorgan Sees Comcast As The Most Logical Buyer
summary: Roku Inc. (NASDAQ:ROKU) shares surged Friday after a report suggested the streaming platform is exploring a potential sale. Now, JPMorgan believes one name stands out from a crowded field of possible buyers. In a note following the report, analyst Cory Carpenter said Roku’s combination of TV operating system ownership and distribution into more than 100 million households makes it a strategic asset. headline: Fox Corp. to buy streaming platform Roku for $22 billion
summary: The deal will combine the Murdoch family's media assets, which include its news, sports and broadcast channels, with the San Jose-based streaming platform company that reaches 100 million consumers globally.

headline: Fox to buy Roku for $22 billion
summary: Fox Corporation plans to buy video streaming giant Roku, along with its namesake channel and customer data, in a deal worth a total of $25 billion.

raw.headline: Fox to buy Roku for $22 billion
raw.summary: Fox Corporation plans to buy video streaming giant Roku, along with its namesake channel and customer data, in a deal worth a total of $25 billion.

headline: Fox to buy streaming pioneer Roku in a $22 billion deal
summary: Fox Corp. has agreed to buy the streaming pioneer Roku in a cash-and-stock deal valued at approximately $22 billion, including debt.  Roku will continue to be run as an open, partner-friendly platform, the companies said Monday, and there appears to be no immediate changes that customers will see.  Fox and Roku said that the combined company will become the third-largest player in U.S. television by share of viewing.
